element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Early enemies establish attack patterns for blocking mechanics
Blocking grants invincibility window for counter-attack; critical for boss vulnerabilities
Arthur's directional slash, Lancelot's jumping kick, Perceval's Giant Swing dash cancel
Screen-clear ability with health cost; risk/reward trade-off
Automatic leveling system; weapons upgrade (Excalibur, sabre, battle-axe); armor visual/stat changes
Horse stomp mechanic; repositions player threat range; Arthur most devastating mounted
Blocking-dependent vulnerabilities; some bosses ride horses; stage-specific mechanics

typical_relief_triggers: Blocking successfully to gain invincibility window
typical_tension_triggers: Guard break (holding block without incoming attacks causes exhaustion)
Health depletion from desperation attack use
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Wave-based enemy encounters with escalating difficulty per stage; boss acts as climactic tension release point; blocking mechanic creates micro-cycles of defense/offense tension

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduction of blocking mechanic; balanced enemies allow mastery of core systems
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Mounted combat segments introduce spatial repositioning; boss patterns require blocking timing precision
difficulty_curve.late_game: Multi-stage bosses; cumulative enemy density; horse-mounted bosses add complexity

notable_level_patterns.description: Generic enemies appear in waves; difficulty escalates per stage through increased enemy count and boss introduction
Each stage concludes with unique boss requiring specific blocking-based vulnerability exploitation
Mid-stage mounted combat segments provide mechanical variety and repositioning dynamics
Automatic stat/weapon/armor progression gates mechanical depth to match difficulty; no player skill/leveling choice
